topic: Integration & impact analysis for 007's 3 gem-team proposals (P1 typed-agent-io-adapter/012, P2 scoped-preexec-and-handoff-gates/013, P3 planner-review-focus-and-drift-hint/014): HOW to integrate each into the live spec-kit + WHICH existing skills/commands/agents/docs it impacts (change-type/severity/backward-compat). Read-only; orchestrator-written state.

noveltyJustification: The main new integration finding is that P1 should be dispatch-input-first, with output normalization mostly additive because `@code`, `@review`, and `@context` already have strong markdown contracts.
P2 is mostly an additive seam-hardening pass; the genuinely new parts are typed receiver validation, scoped boundary contract-first ordering, and a medium/high pre-mortem field.
The main new integration finding is that P3 can avoid MCP/schema churn by mapping drift into existing `handover_state` plus compact `_memory.continuity` fields.
The main new cross-cutting finding is that governance and validators mostly stay untouched; the clean integration point is one optional workflow contract plus small agent-doc adapters.

focus: RQ5 command/workflow blast radius + rollout sequencing
noveltyJustification: The main new finding is that `/speckit:complete` is the riskiest rollout target because it duplicates plan/implement behavior and has a review-gate/Step-11 mismatch.

verdict: feasible & fully additive; one shared agent-io-contract + @orchestrate hub; 4-wave rollout; smallest-first = P1 Wave 1
